articles.title: Maruti Suzuki Launches India’s First WagonR Flex Fuel Car At ₹7.24 Lakh
articles.description: Maruti Suzuki has launched the WagonR Flex Fuel at ₹7.24 lakh, marking India’s first flex-fuel passenger car. The model can run on ethanol blends from E20 to E100 and uses a 1.2-litre petrol engine. It comes with standard WagonR features and strengthens Maruti’s multi-powertrain portfolio including EV, hybrid, CNG, and petrol

articles.title: European Markets Soar as U.S.-Iran Agreement Boosts STOXX 600
articles.description: Europe's STOXX 600 reached a new high following a preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, which positively affected global risk sentiment and reduced oil prices. The index recouped all conflict-related losses, narrowing its gap with the U.S. S&P 500. Energy and luxury stocks saw significant gains amid easing tensions.

articles.title: Wholesale inflation surges to 9.68% in May over sharp rise in crude oil prices
articles.description: The wholesale price inflation climbed to a series high of 9.68% in May 2026 under the new 2022-23 base year, up from 8.26% in April, as fuel and crude petroleum prices surged sharply. Easing West Asia tensions may cool energy prices and offer some relief in the June WPI print.

articles.title: Asian assets extend early rally as US-Iran peace deal eases oil fears
articles.description: Emerging Asian equities and currencies extended gains from early trade on Monday, with Philippine shares climbing nearly 7%, as a preliminary U.S.-Iran peace agreement brought oil prices down,...

articles.title: UPDATE 1-UK bond yields fall to two-month low on US-Iran preliminary deal
articles.description: British government bond yields dropped to a two-month low on Monday. This followed a preliminary peace deal between the United States and Iran. Oil prices fell more than five percent. Two-year gilt yields dropped over eight basis points. Ten-year yields also fell. This eased fears of a wider stagflationary shock. Investors became dovish on future rates outlook.

articles.title: West Asia conflict: From oil to inflation, how did the crisis impact India?
articles.description: The West Asia crisis affected India through higher oil prices, pressure on the rupee, weaker trade, rising aviation costs and inflation. Here's how the economy absorbed the shock

articles.title: Traders Cut South Africa Interest Rate
articles.description: Traders have scaled back bets on additional South African interest-rate hikes after a US-Iran peace deal sent oil prices tumbling, potentially reducing inflationary pressures.

articles.title: Dollar Dips as U.S.-Iran Peace Prospects Brighten
articles.description: The U.S. dollar slipped to a 10-day low amid a tentative U.S.-Iran peace deal that boosted risk assets and oil prices plummeted. Officials plan to sign the framework agreement on Friday, though nuclear program issues remain unresolved. Central banks focus on inflation as interest rate decisions loom.

articles.title: Crude Oil Below $70 Can Help Rupee Recover To 92.5 Level, Ease Import Bill
articles.description: Analysts predict that falling oil prices will alleviate India's import expenses and current account issues, potentially allowing the rupee to strengthen further over the coming months., Economy, Times Now
